handoff.summary: Visual polish applied: palette consolidation (12 room + 6 avatar + 6 status + 6 shell-chrome CSS custom properties), VS Code theme integration (dark/light/high-contrast via vscode-* body classes and CSS variable fallbacks), animation easing refined to ease-in-out, prefers-reduced-motion compliance at both CSS media-query and JS levels, focus indicators using --focus-ring everywhere, font-size minimum raised to 10px, contain: layout paint on room/desk/avatar for repaint isolation, loading and empty state visual treatment.

change_details.description: Palette consolidation: expanded :root with --skin, --pants, --shoe, --provider-copilot/codex/unassigned, --shell-bg/fg/border, --focus-ring. Theme integration: body background adapts via body.vscode-dark/light/high-contrast rules; .hud/.legend get light and HC overrides. Font sizes: bumped 8-9px to 10px minimum for .desk__status, .desk__agent, .desk-overlay__chip, .desk-menu__chip, .desk-menu__button, .desk-overlay__link. Focus rings: all :focus-visible selectors use var(--focus-ring). Performance: contain: layout style on .room, contain: layout paint on .desk. Reduced motion: CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Loading/empty: new .loading-state and .empty-state CSS; renderScene shows loading spinner before data arrives and empty state when no desks.

Avatar palette: replaced hardcoded #f1c58a with var(--skin), #5f92ff with var(--active), #2d3d5e with var(--pants), #2a2026 with var(--shoe), provider colors with var(--provider-*). Animation easing: changed avatar-work/walk/pin from steps(2, end) to ease-in-out for smoother motion; avatar-work reduced from 700ms to 600ms. Font size: badge and provider labels bumped from 8px to 10px. Performance: contain: layout paint on .avatar. Reduced motion: added CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block alongside JS .room--reduced-motion class.

notes: All changes are visual-only refinements. No data model, message protocol, or feature changes. The room's decorative pixel-art palette (12 colors) stays fixed across themes; only the shell chrome (hud, legend, viewport frame, body background) adapts to VS Code's theme via CSS variable fallbacks.
